nodes.label: CHECK_ERROR must never be reported to the operator as a FAIL finding

Classify a pool node whose NodeRecord is absent from the snapshot as known-down,
counting it toward quorum loss even when DataIncomplete=true.

Return a CRITICAL or ERROR finding when a rule hits InvariantStateCheckError
(query failed, proto parse error, etcd unreachable). CHECK_ERROR is indeterminate —
it is not evidence of failure.

Document "restart all controllers in sequence after every objectstore apply" as the
canonical procedure, instead of fixing the apply-watcher goroutine recovery.

Use PrimaryIP() or Ips[0] directly when matching a node against pool member IPs,
without excluding the cluster VIP. Returns wrong results when the node holds the VIP.
The objectstore apply-watcher goroutine silently dies on etcd compaction, leaving apply_request keys unprocessed for hours
Partial objectstore snapshot fires false CRITICAL quorum-loss finding
Floating VIP appears as pool member IP in objectstore rule, causing the VIP-holder to be misclassified
The cluster VIP must not appear as a pool member IP in objectstore, etcd, or ScyllaDB membership checks
Missing inventory in a partial objectstore snapshot must be treated as unknown, not known-down
Each MinIO pool drive must map to a unique physical storage path; no two pool nodes may share the same physical bytes
MinIO topology must be read from etcd ObjectStoreDesiredState, not inferred from local disk or installed records
